skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations.
skill.summary: Generate AI images with 50+ models including FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream via inference.sh CLI with text-to-image, upscaling, and editing capabilities.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 47 patterns (34 external_commands, 11 network URLs, 2 blockers). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES: markdown code blocks were misidentified as Ruby backtick execution, documentation URLs flagged as network risks, YAML frontmatter flagged as weak crypto, and legitimate CLI install pattern flagged as pipe-to-shell. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code. Low risk assigned due to dependency on external CLI service from third-party domain.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples, not Ruby backtick execution. Static analyzer misidentified backticks in markdown as shell execution.
Documentation URLs linking to inference.sh resources, images, and documentation pages. Not malicious endpoints or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External CLI Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires installation and use of third-party CLI tool (inference.sh) from external domain. Users must trust the CLI vendor for security and data handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear dependency on external service - install command downloads CLI from cli.inference.sh and all image generation requests route through inference.sh platform.

content.value_statement: Create professional AI images without complex setup or multiple subscriptions. Access FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream and more through a unified CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 50+ AI models including FLUX, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok Imagine, and Seedream 4.5
Edit existing images with natural language instructions using Reve model
Upscale low-resolution images to 4K quality with Topaz upscaler
Apply LoRA styles for consistent character and brand aesthetics
Create images with accurate text rendering for posters and graphics
Stitch multiple images together horizontally or vertically
limitations: Requires installation of inference.sh CLI and account registration
Image generation is a paid service - credits required for API usage
Cannot generate images locally - all processing occurs on inference.sh platform
Output quality and speed depend on selected model and server load

use_cases.title: Marketing and Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Create product mockups, social media graphics, and marketing visuals without photography or design software.

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Prototyping
use_cases.description: Rapidly visualize ideas for games, films, or product designs with text-to-image generation.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Images
use_cases.description: Generate lifestyle images showing products in various settings without physical photoshoots.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of: [describe your subject, setting, and style]. Use the FLUX model for best results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner users creating their first AI image

prompt_templates.title: Product Photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of [product name] with [lighting style] lighting on [background]. Shot with studio quality, commercial photography style.

prompt_templates.title: Style-Specific Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [subject] in the style of [artist/art movement]. Use [color palette] colors with [mood] atmosphere. Aspect ratio [16:9 or 9:16].

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing with Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image to [describe changes]. Add text that says [text content] in [font style]. Ensure the text is clearly readable and naturally integrated.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced users working with Reve for text rendering

output_examples.input: Generate a photorealistic image of a coffee mug on a wooden table with morning sunlight
output_examples.output: Returns a high-resolution product photography style image showing a ceramic coffee mug positioned on natural wood grain surface with soft directional lighting creating gentle shadows

output_examples.input: Create a cyberpunk cityscape at night with neon lights and flying vehicles, 16:9 aspect ratio
output_examples.output: Produces a wide cinematic image featuring futuristic urban architecture with glowing neon signage in purple and blue tones, aircraft trails between skyscrapers, and rain-slicked streets reflecting city lights

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts with clear subject, style, lighting, and composition details for best results
Start with FLUX or Seedream models for general purpose generation, then experiment with specialized models for specific needs
Include aspect ratio specifications (16:9, 9:16, 1:1) based on your intended use case (video, social media, print)
anti_patterns: Do not use vague single-word prompts like 'cat' - describe the scene, style, and mood for usable results
Avoid requesting copyrighted character names or trademarked content that may violate usage policies
Do not expect photorealistic human faces to be perfect - AI may produce artifacts in hands, eyes, and teeth

faq.question: Do I need to pay to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but image generation requires credits on the inference.sh platform. Check their pricing page for current rates per model and resolution.

faq.question: Which model should I use for my project?
faq.answer: FLUX Dev for versatile quality, Seedream 4.5 for cinematic 4K images, Gemini for Google integration, Reve for text rendering, and Topaz for upscaling existing images.

faq.question: Can I use generated images commercially?
faq.answer: Check the inference.sh terms of service and individual model licenses. Most models allow commercial use, but verify restrictions before using in client work.

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run: curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh && infsh login. The script detects your OS, downloads the binary, and verifies its checksum automatically.

faq.question: What image resolutions are available?
faq.answer: Resolution depends on the model. Seedream supports 2K-4K outputs, FLUX offers various aspect ratios, and Topaz can upscale any image to higher resolutions.

faq.question: Can I edit images I already have?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Reve model for natural language image editing or Topaz for upscaling. Provide the image URL and describe the changes you want.
